steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content:
A decrease of $18\%$ means $82\%$ of the mass remains after each period.
$$100\%-18\%=82\%=0.82$$

steps.title: Count the decay periods
steps.content:
Each period lasts $9.42$ minutes, so after $t$ minutes the number of decay periods is
$$\frac{t}{9.42}$$

steps.title: Match the exponential model
steps.content: The model must use base $0.82$ and exponent $$\frac{t}{9.42}$$ The matching equation is $$M=120(0.82)^{\frac{t}{9.42}}$$ The answer is A.
